name: Howie
description: Email-native AI scheduling assistant that coordinates meeting times, updates calendars, and follows up automatically, backed by a human-in-the-loop review layer.

disruptionMechanisms: Narrow-scope reliability versus broad AI-assistant bundles
attributes.goToMarket: Product-led with viral email CC mechanic; SMB and prosumer self-serve
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with human-in-the-loop review layer
attributes.problemStatement: Professionals with high external-meeting volume waste significant time manually negotiating and coordinating meeting scheduling.
attributes.productDescription: An AI assistant that users CC on email threads to autonomously negotiate meeting times, add calendar events, follow up with participants, and flag schedule conflicts, backed by human reviewers who correct errors before they reach the customer.

founders.name: Austin Petersmith
founders.background: Previously VP Growth and Community at Vendr; co-prototyped an AI scheduling assistant with Dave Newman in 2015 before founding Howie once the technology matured.

founders.name: Dave Newman
founders.background: Worked with Petersmith at Assembly starting in 2013; co-founded Howie after a decade-long collaboration on the scheduling-automation idea.

name: Chronicle
description: AI presentation maker that converts documents, notes, and business context into editable, branded slide decks on a free-form canvas.

technicalApproaches: Generative AI for design and layout
founderArchetypes: Domain expert founder (decade in presentation consulting)
disruptionMechanisms: Competes with legacy office suites via AI-native generation
attributes.goToMarket: Product-led, organic/viral (100k-user waitlist with zero paid marketing)
attributes.problemStatement: Building professional, narrative-driven presentations from raw notes and data is slow and design-skill-dependent.
attributes.productDescription: An AI presentation tool that turns documents, notes, and data into editable, branded decks with AI-driven research, narrative structuring, and design execution, including live 'Peek' and 'Deep Hover' presentation modes.

founders.name: Mayuresh Patole
founders.background: Former management consultant and designer with roughly a decade of focus on presentations, per Forbes.

founders.name: Tejas Gawande
founders.background: Brings social-media and growth-strategy expertise, per Forbes; company site cites a founding team with McKinsey, BCG, and Apple backgrounds.

name: Fyxer
description: AI executive-assistant SaaS that organizes inboxes, drafts email replies, and produces meeting notes, trained on real executive-assistant workflow data.

founderArchetypes: Operator-turned-founder (ran a real executive-assistant agency pre-software)
disruptionMechanisms: Competes with bundled Google/Microsoft AI features
regulatoryLabels: Handles email and calendar personal data
attributes.goToMarket: Product-led self-serve plus enterprise sales at scale
attributes.problemStatement: Professionals spend significant time on inbox triage, email drafting, and meeting note-taking that a trained AI assistant can automate.
attributes.productDescription: An AI executive assistant that organizes inboxes, drafts email replies, and produces meeting notes and summaries, differentiated by training data drawn from the founders' prior real-world executive-assistant agency.

founders.name: Richard Hollingsworth
founders.background: Co-built the UK's largest executive-assistant agency with his brother, bootstrapping it to $5m revenue, before founding Fyxer.

founders.name: Archie Hollingsworth
founders.background: Co-built the original bootstrapped executive-assistant agency with Richard before the pivot to Fyxer.

founders.name: Matt Ffrench
founders.background: Technical co-founder who used roughly 500,000 hours of executive-assistant workflow data from the original agency to train Fyxer's product.

name: Napkin
description: Text-to-visual generation platform that converts written content into diagrams, charts, and infographics for business storytelling.

founderArchetypes: Repeat founders with a prior exit (Osmo)
disruptionMechanisms: Reduces manual design labor for business visuals
Competes with prompt-based AI image generators
attributes.problemStatement: Knowledge workers need to communicate ideas visually but lack design skill, time, or prompt-engineering fluency to create diagrams and infographics.
attributes.productDescription: A web-based tool that transforms text into customizable visuals -- diagrams, flowcharts, infographics, Venn diagrams, and decision trees -- using a generation-first interaction model rather than requiring users to describe a desired image.

founders.name: Pramod Sharma
founders.background: Ex-Google engineer; previously founded and sold Osmo, an educational-games company.

founders.name: Jerome Scholler
founders.background: Was on Osmo's founding team with Sharma; previously worked at Ubisoft, LucasArts, and Google.

name: Ello
description: AI reading coach for children ages 4-9 that listens to a child read aloud from physical books and provides real-time correction using child-specific speech recognition.

founderArchetypes: Cross-disciplinary team (education operator, clinical psychologist, AI researcher)
Repeat founder with prior ML exits (Voss)
disruptionMechanisms: Targets literacy gap underserved by generic edtech
attributes.businessModel: Consumer subscription (app plus mailed physical books)
attributes.goToMarket: Consumer direct-to-parent subscription; school/district site licensing
attributes.problemStatement: Many young children lack access to consistent, one-on-one reading practice and correction, contributing to childhood literacy gaps.
attributes.productDescription: A subscription product combining a mobile app with child-specific AI speech recognition and a monthly mailing of physical books, designed to give children real-time reading coaching similar to a parent or tutor sitting beside them.

founders.name: Tom Sayer
founders.background: Previously led impact and adoption programs at Google for Education.

founders.name: Elizabeth Adams
founders.background: Clinical psychologist specializing in child development and behavior.

founders.name: Catalin Voss
founders.background: AI researcher and PhD candidate at Stanford; co-founded and sold two prior ML startups, DukaConnect and Sension.

name: SchoolAI
description: K-12 classroom AI platform combining teacher-facing AI assistants with student-facing personalized AI tutors and lessons, with full FERPA/COPPA/SOC2/ESSA compliance positioning.

founderArchetypes: Prior edtech product leadership (Apple, Lambda School)
disruptionMechanisms: Automates lesson prep and classroom administrative work
Personalizes instruction at scale beyond one teacher's bandwidth
attributes.businessModel: Freemium to paid tiered SaaS (Free, Pro, Scale) plus district contracts
attributes.goToMarket: Bottoms-up teacher adoption expanding into district-level enterprise sales
attributes.problemStatement: Teachers face shrinking budgets, teacher shortages, and growing class sizes that make personalized attention for every student difficult to deliver.
attributes.productDescription: A K-12 AI platform pairing teacher-facing AI assistants for classroom prep and administrative work with 'Spaces' -- personalized AI tutors, games, and lessons that adapt to each student's learning style -- built with a full education-compliance certification stack.

founders.name: Caleb Hicks
founders.background: 15-plus years in education as a classroom teacher and product leader, including developing iOS curriculum at Apple and co-founding Lambda School.

name: Lindy
description: No-code platform for building and managing AI agents that automate SMB business workflows including inbox, calendar, CRM, and support tasks.

targetCustomers: Sales, marketing, operations, support, and recruiting teams
founderArchetypes: Pivoted founder (rebuilt company from a prior startup, TeamFlow)
disruptionMechanisms: Extends no-code automation into autonomous agent execution
attributes.goToMarket: Product-led self-serve (viral launch) plus direct sales for enterprise
attributes.problemStatement: SMB teams need to automate repetitive cross-application workflows (inbox, CRM, scheduling, support) without engineering resources.
attributes.productDescription: A no-code AI agent builder that automates business workflows across connected apps -- inbox management, meeting scheduling, CRM updates, and ad hoc tasks -- targeted at SMBs and startups.

founders.name: Flo Crivello
founders.background: Founded TeamFlow, then pivoted the company to Lindy in early 2023 after recognizing an AI-agent opportunity in a customer's request to automate CRM updates; cut the team by roughly two-thirds during the pivot to preserve capital.

round: Series A (stage and financing history disputed across public sources; no confirmed round since 2023)

name: Delphi
description: Platform that lets experts and creators build self-updating 'digital minds' trained on their own content, enabling audiences to call, text, or video-chat with an AI trained to match the creator's voice and thinking.

targetCustomers: Content creators with an existing audience
technicalApproaches: LLM fine-tuning/RAG on creator content corpus
Voice, text, and video conversational interface
founderArchetypes: Repeat founder with a prior exit (Friday)
disruptionMechanisms: Shifts creator monetization from one-to-many content to one-to-one interactive access at scale
attributes.businessModel: Tiered subscription SaaS (Free, Builder, Scaler, Immortal)
attributes.goToMarket: Creator-led adoption; direct outreach to established experts and public figures
attributes.problemStatement: Experts and creators cannot personally scale one-on-one interaction with their full audience, limiting how much of their accumulated knowledge and time can be monetized.
attributes.productDescription: A platform where creators link their existing content (writing, video, podcasts, books) to a self-updating AI 'digital mind' that audiences can call, text, or video-chat with, matching the creator's speaking and thinking style.

founders.name: Dara Ladjevardian
founders.background: Previously founded and sold Friday, an AI shopping assistant; worked as a Forward Deployed Engineer at C3 AI and as a product engineer at OpenStore.

founders.name: Sam Spelsberg
founders.background: Co-built Delphi's technical architecture with Ladjevardian.

name: Happenstance
description: AI-powered people-search tool that indexes a user's own network across email, calendar, and social platforms into a searchable relationship graph.

technicalApproaches: Embedding search over aggregated contact data
OAuth-based multi-source data aggregation (Gmail, LinkedIn, X, Instagram, Outlook)
founderArchetypes: Technical PhD founder (Stanford CS, autonomous-vehicle perception background)
disruptionMechanisms: Surfaces warm-introduction paths not visible on LinkedIn alone
Aggregates fragmented personal-network data into one searchable index
attributes.businessModel: Freemium with paid 'Pro seats' (exact pricing undisclosed)
attributes.problemStatement: Professionals' networks are fragmented across email, calendar, and social platforms, making it hard to find and act on warm connections for recruiting, sales, or fundraising.
attributes.productDescription: An AI tool that connects to a user's Gmail, Calendar, LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, and Outlook accounts to build a searchable relationship graph, returning natural-language search results ranked by relevance, relationship strength, and recency.

founders.name: Alex Teichman
founders.background: Stanford CS PhD who worked on perception systems for self-driving cars; Y Combinator Winter 2024 batch founder backed by Elad Gil and Garry Tan.

round: Seed (exact amount disputed across public sources: $500k-$2.5m range reported)

name: Brisk Teaching
description: Browser-native AI copilot for K-12 educators that embeds into existing tools (Google Docs, Slides, Canvas) to generate lesson plans, feedback, and classroom activities.

technicalApproaches: Browser-extension embedding into existing edtech tools
founderArchetypes: Policy and big-tech cross-background founder (White House OCTO, Google, CZI Education)
disruptionMechanisms: Automates lesson planning, grading feedback, and activity creation
Embeds into incumbent tools rather than replacing them
attributes.goToMarket: Bottoms-up individual teacher adoption via browser extension, expanding to district contracts
attributes.problemStatement: K-12 teachers spend significant unpaid time on lesson planning, differentiation, and feedback that could be automated within tools they already use.
attributes.productDescription: A browser-native AI copilot that layers into Google Docs, Slides, Canvas, and similar tools to generate lesson plans, differentiate reading levels, draft feedback and report-card comments, and create quizzes and student-facing activities.

founders.name: Arman Jaffer
founders.background: Previously worked in the White House Office of the Chief Technology Officer, on Google's mobile search team, and as a product manager on the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative's Education team; Forbes 30 Under 30 Education honoree.
